# Aura FHE

> Aura FHE is the encrypted compute layer for the Solana Virtual Machine. It runs Solana programs on data that stays sealed end-to-end using fully homomorphic encryption, delivering privacy as a mathematical guarantee rather than a policy.

Aura is developed by Mochi Labs (Singapore HQ) on more than a decade of cryptographic research. The protocol uses a LUT-FHE (lookup-table FHE) architecture that defeats the bootstrapping bottleneck — integer addition at ~0.04μs, ~0.1s verification, and ~50 tokens/second encrypted LLM inference on consumer hardware. Security is based on MQ-hardness (multivariate quadratic problems, proven NP-hard), giving 128-bit post-quantum security.

Aura is blockchain-agnostic by design but is shipping first on Solana. It operates as a coprocessor — no chain migration, no trusted execution environments, no threshold networks. The SDK exposes three primitives (encrypt, compute, decrypt) so a Solana developer can ship a fully confidential application in under an hour.

**Important disclaimer:** Aura FHE is not available to citizens or residents of the United States of America or the People's Republic of China.

## Core pages

- [Homepage](https://afhe.io/): Overview of the encrypted compute layer for Solana.
- [Technology](https://afhe.io/technology): LUT-FHE architecture, the bootstrapping bottleneck, MQ-hardness security model, performance benchmarks.
- [Ecosystem](https://afhe.io/ecosystem): Live applications (AuraPoly sealed-bid prediction markets), confidential AI inference, institutional RWA, AURA SDK v5.
- [About](https://afhe.io/about): Mochi Labs, team, mission, principles, roadmap.
- [Contact](https://afhe.io/contact): Partnerships, press, integration support.

## Documentation

- [Docs landing](https://afhe.io/docs): In-site documentation hub.
- [Whitepaper](https://afhe.io/docs/whitepaper): Full technical whitepaper, ten sections covering protocol, tokenomics, roadmap, and security.

## Blog

- [Engineering blog](https://afhe.io/blog): Notes on FHE, private DeFi, and the cryptography behind Aura.

## Key entities

- **Aura FHE** — the protocol (also referred to as AFHE or Aura Fully Homomorphic Encryption).
- **Mochi Labs** — the company building Aura.
- **AuraPoly** — flagship production application: sealed-bid prediction markets running on the Aura encrypted compute layer (https://aurapoly.com/).
- **ZFHE (ZionFHE)** — the underlying cryptographic scheme behind Aura's LUT-FHE.
- **AURA token** — used for staking (coprocessor network security), governance (veAURA), compute fees, and mining rewards. Total supply 10B; 50% distributed via fair PoW mining with Bitcoin-style halving.

## Comparison points

- vs Zama TFHE — Aura is ~84,500× faster on CNN inference benchmarks, with stronger security (MQ-hardness proven NP-hard vs LWE believed-hard) and no threshold network dependency.
- vs Microsoft CKKS — Aura is ~13,500× faster on CNN inference benchmarks.
- vs zero-knowledge proofs — ZK proves a computation happened correctly; FHE performs the computation directly on encrypted data. They are complementary, not substitutes.

## Roadmap (high level)

- Q1 2026 — Token launch and veAURA staking.
- Q2 2026 — Mining network launch; wallet beta with SDK integrations.
- Q3 2026 — Mainnet with DEX integration.
- Q4 2026 — AURA DEX beta with encrypted orderbook.
- 2027+ — Native L1 blockchain.
